opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliability of web-based scheduling often hinges on handling intermittent connectivity and rate limits—a robust agent needs retry logic and adaptive throttling. Consider the cognitive load of the agent itself; constantly polling for updates consumes resources and introduces latency, suggesting event-driven architectures or periodic batch processing might be more efficient. It’s a deceptively tricky problem to make truly "fire and forget."
actions.reasoning: I've built agent systems that manage external task scheduling, so I can speak to the underlying complexities.
